title: Day 1 — Virtual species with nicheR
text: Workshop navigation — part 1 4 ENM-Thailand 2026 workshop. Day 1 (page) — Virtual species nicheR. Build ellipsoid niche hand project . Day 2a — Downloading workshop data. Fetch Thailand, WorldClim GBIF Sambar records. Day 2b — Environmental thinning bean. Reduce E-space sampling bias fitting real niche. Day 3 — Temporally-explicit SDMs TemporalModelR. Pair occurrence environment experienced. nicheR R package building visualising ellipsoid-based ecological niches environmental space (E-space) projecting onto geographic space (G-space). Today treat niche hypothesis control — virtual species — never need real occurrence data. ideal way learn ENM mechanics, can compare every output back known truth. Today : Build ellipsoid user-defined range bioclimatic tolerances. Explore environmental space Thailand 3D using BIO1, BIO12, BIO15. Project ellipsoid onto Thailand bioclim rasters get suitability Mahalanobis distance. Add sampling-bias layer sample virtual occurrence points using three sampling strategies (centroid, random, edge).

title: Day 1 — Virtual species with nicheR
text: chunk installs everything Day 1 needs first time run . Re-running safe — already-installed packages skipped.
code: # ---- CRAN dependencies ----------------------------------------------------- cran_pkgs <- c(   "terra",   "sf",   "plotly",   "viridis",   "rmarkdown",   "knitr",   "remotes" ) to_install <- cran_pkgs[!cran_pkgs %in% rownames(installed.packages())] if (length(to_install)) install.packages(to_install, dependencies = TRUE)  # ---- nicheR from GitHub ---------------------------------------------------- if (!requireNamespace("nicheR", quietly = TRUE)) {   remotes::install_github("castanedaM/nicheR", upgrade = "never") } library(nicheR) library(terra) library(sf) library(plotly)  # Reproducible random draws throughout the lesson set.seed(2026)

title: Day 1 — Virtual species with nicheR
text: Day 1 work WorldClim bioclim rasters Thailand polygon. files produced Day 2a (Day2a_Data_Download.Rmd) — please knit Day 2a Day 1 files exist data/processed/. clean teaching example work three bioclimatic variables. also drive 3-D environmental exploration next step: bio1 — annual mean temperature (°C, WorldClim v2.1) bio12 — annual precipitation (mm) bio15 — precipitation seasonality (CV)
code: bio_path <- "data/processed/bioclim/bioclim_thailand.tif" tha_path <- "data/processed/boundary/thailand_boundary.gpkg"  bioclim <- rast(bio_path) thailand <- st_read(tha_path, quiet = TRUE)  plot(thailand[2])  # Show layer names — bio1 … bio19 names(bioclim)  summary(bioclim) env3    <- bioclim[[c("bio1", "bio12", "bio15")]] env3_df <- as.data.frame(env3, na.rm = TRUE) plot(env3)

what: Step 1 — Explore E-space in 3D (BIO1 × BIO12 × BIO15)
title: Day 1 — Virtual species with nicheR
text: defining niche, look available environmental conditions Thailand three dimensions. BIO15 (precipitation seasonality, CV) captures within-year rainfall variability adds important third axis top mean temperature (BIO1) total annual precipitation (BIO12).
code: plotly::plot_ly(   data   = env3_df,   x      = ~bio1, y = ~bio12, z = ~bio15,   type   = "scatter3d", mode = "markers",   marker = list(size = 2, opacity = 0.5) ) |>   plotly::layout(scene = list(     xaxis = list(title = "BIO1 (°C)"),     yaxis = list(title = "BIO12 (mm)"),     zaxis = list(title = "BIO15 (CV)")   ))

what: Step 2 — Define a virtual species’ niche
title: Day 1 — Virtual species with nicheR
text: nicheR ellipsoid built range data frame lists minimum maximum environmental variable can take inside species’ niche. package converts centroid, covariance matrix, semi-axes, niche volume. Imagine hypothetical tropical mammal tolerates mean annual temperatures 25 28 °C annual precipitation 1200 2500 mm. ell carries centroid, covariance matrix, dimensions niche. can visualise directly E-space, available environmental conditions Thailand background.
code: range_df <- data.frame(   bio1  = c(25, 28),   bio12 = c(1200, 2500),   bio15 = c(70, 90) )  ell <- build_ellipsoid(range = range_df) ell # `env3_df` from Step 1 carries every pixel's (bio1, bio12, bio15) values — # we use it as the background point cloud here too. plot_ellipsoid(ell, background = env3_df)

what: Step 3 — Predict suitability and Mahalanobis distance in G-space
title: Day 1 — Virtual species with nicheR
text: predict() projects ellipsoid onto SpatRaster returns suitability surface (1 centroid, 0 boundary, truncated = TRUE clamping outside-niche pixels zero) Mahalanobis distance surface.
code: pred <- predict(ell,                 newdata = env3,                 include_suitability = TRUE,                 suitability_truncated = TRUE,                 include_mahalanobis = TRUE,                 mahalanobis_truncated = TRUE,                 keep_data = TRUE)  # pred is a multi-layer SpatRaster carrying "suitability" and "mahalanobis" pred  plot(pred,      col  = viridis::viridis(100))  # Binary potential distribution map binary_rast <- (pred$suitability_trunc > 0) * 1  plot(binary_rast,      axes = FALSE, box = TRUE,      col  = c("#d4d4d4", "#0004d5"),      main = "Potential Distribution (Binary)")

title: Day 1 — Virtual species with nicheR
text: Real-world surveys biased toward accessible places. can simulate bias apply top suitability. lesson use species-richness nighttime-lights rasters (written Day2a_Data_Download.Rmd) proxies survey effort.
code: sp_rich_layer   <- rast("data/processed/bias/sp_richness_TH.tif") nighttime_layer <- rast("data/processed/bias/nighttime_TH.tif") bias_layer <- c(sp_rich_layer, nighttime_layer) plot(bias_layer)  # Combine bias layers into a 0-1 composite surface bias_prep <- prepare_bias(bias_surface = bias_layer,                           effect_direction = c("direct", "inverse"))  plot(bias_prep$composite_surface) biased_pred <- apply_bias(prepared_bias = bias_prep,                           prediction = pred,                           prediction_layer = "suitability",                           effect_direction = "direct")  plot(biased_pred$suitability_biased,      main = "Suitability × bias  =  realised sampling surface") plot(pred$suitability)

what: Step 5 — Generate virtual occurrence points with three sampling strategies
title: Day 1 — Virtual species with nicheR
text: nicheR::sample_data() accepts three sampling strategies: "centroid" — points weighted toward niche centroid (preferred conditions). "random" — points drawn uniformly inside niche. "edge" — points biased toward niche periphery (marginal conditions). draw 50 virtual records strategy compare . bias-weighted draw uses sample_biased_data() together realised sampling surface. three strategies populate niche differently E-space:
code: pts_centroid <- sample_data(n_occ = 50,                             prediction = pred,                             sampling = "centroid",                             prediction_layer = "suitability",                             seed = 123)  pts_random   <- sample_data(n_occ = 50,                             prediction = pred,                             sampling = "random",                             prediction_layer = "suitability",                             seed = 123)  pts_edge     <- sample_data(n_occ = 50,                             prediction = pred,                             sampling = "edge",                             prediction_layer = "suitability",                             seed = 123)  head(pts_centroid) head(pts_random) head(pts_edge) biased_pts <- sample_biased_data(   n_occ            = 50,   prediction       = biased_pred,   prediction_layer = "suitability_biased_direct",   seed             = 123 )  head(biased_pts)  occ_bias_env <- terra::extract(env3, biased_pts[, c("x", "y")]) par(mfrow = c(2, 2))  plot(pred[["suitability"]],      main = "Centroid sample (n = 50)") points(pts_centroid[, c("x", "y")], pch = 19, cex = 0.6, col = "red")  plot(pred[["suitability"]],      main = "Random sample (n = 50)") points(pts_random[, c("x", "y")], pch = 19, cex = 0.6, col = "red")  plot(pred[["suitability"]],      main = "Edge sample (n = 50)") points(pts_edge[, c("x", "y")], pch = 19, cex = 0.6, col = "red")  plot(biased_pred$suitability_biased,      main = "Bias-weighted sample (n = 50)") points(biased_pts[, c("x", "y")], pch = 19, cex = 0.6, col = "red")  par(mfrow = c(1, 1)) plot_ellipsoid(ell, background = env3_df) points(pts_centroid[, c("bio1", "bio12")], col = "blue",      pch = 19, cex = 0.6) points(pts_random[,   c("bio1", "bio12")], col = "darkgreen", pch = 17, cex = 0.6) points(pts_edge[,     c("bio1", "bio12")], col = "purple",    pch = 15, cex = 0.6) points(occ_bias_env[, c("bio1", "bio12")], col = "red",       pch = 4,  cex = 0.6) legend("topright",        legend = c("Centroid", "Random", "Edge", "Bias-weighted"),        col    = c("blue", "darkgreen", "purple", "red"),        pch    = c(19, 17, 15, 4),        bty    = "n")

title: Day 1 — Virtual species with nicheR
text: just : Defined niche priori 2-D ellipsoid bioclim space. Explored available environmental space Thailand 3D using BIO1, BIO12, BIO15. Projected ellipsoid Thailand inspected suitability + Mahalanobis distance. Added sampling-bias surface contrasted three sampling strategies (centroid, random, edge) plus bias-weighted draw.

title: Day 2a — Downloading the workshop data
text: Workshop navigation — part 2 4 ENM-Thailand 2026 workshop. Day 1 — Virtual species nicheR. Build ellipsoid niche hand project . Day 2a (page) — Downloading workshop data. Fetch Thailand, WorldClim GBIF Sambar records. Day 2b — Environmental thinning bean. Reduce E-space sampling bias fitting real niche. Day 3 — Temporally-explicit SDMs TemporalModelR. Pair occurrence environment experienced. notebook gathers every input rest workshop needs. Knit start Day 2 — everything else (Day 1, Day 2b, Day 3) reads data/processed/. download / prepare: Thailand national boundary (GADM v4.1). WorldClim v2.1 bioclim (10 arc-min, 19 layers, cropped + masked Thailand). GBIF occurrence records Sambar deer (Rusa unicolor) Thailand, strictly filtered basisOfRecord %% c("HUMAN_OBSERVATION", "OCCURRENCE", "OBSERVATION"). Two bias proxies (species richness, night-time lights) used Day 1.

title: Day 2a — Downloading the workshop data
code: cran_pkgs <- c(   "terra",   "sf",   "geodata",   "rgbif",   "dplyr",   "readr",   "rmarkdown",   "knitr",   "remotes" ) to_install <- cran_pkgs[!cran_pkgs %in% rownames(installed.packages())] if (length(to_install)) install.packages(to_install, dependencies = TRUE) library(terra) library(sf) library(geodata) library(rgbif) library(dplyr) library(readr) raw_dir       <- "data/raw" processed_dir <- "data/processed"  # data/processed/ is organised into role-based subfolders. boundary_dir <- file.path(processed_dir, "boundary")   # Thailand polygon bioclim_dir  <- file.path(processed_dir, "bioclim")    # WorldClim layers bias_dir     <- file.path(processed_dir, "bias")       # sampling-bias proxies (Day 1) gbif_dir     <- file.path(processed_dir, "gbif")       # GBIF Sambar records  for (d in c(raw_dir, processed_dir,             boundary_dir, bioclim_dir, bias_dir, gbif_dir)) {   dir.create(d, recursive = TRUE, showWarnings = FALSE) }

title: Day 2a — Downloading the workshop data
code: tha <- gadm(country = "THA", level = 0, path = raw_dir) tha_sf <- st_as_sf(tha)  st_write(tha_sf,              file.path(boundary_dir, "thailand_boundary.gpkg"),              delete_dsn = TRUE, quiet = TRUE)  plot(st_geometry(tha_sf), main = "Thailand boundary (GADM level 0)")

title: Day 2a — Downloading the workshop data
code: bio_global <- worldclim_global(var = "bio", res = 10, path = raw_dir)  bio_tha <- crop(bio_global, terra::ext(tha)) bio_tha <- mask(bio_tha, tha)  # Tidy names: wc2.1_10m_bio_1 -> bio1 names(bio_tha) <- paste0("bio", seq_len(terra::nlyr(bio_tha)))  writeRaster(   bio_tha,   filename  = file.path(bioclim_dir, "bioclim_thailand.tif"),   overwrite = TRUE )  bio_tha <- rast(file.path(bioclim_dir, "bioclim_thailand.tif")) plot(bio_tha[[c("bio1", "bio12", "bio15")]],      main = c("BIO1 — mean T (°C)",               "BIO12 — annual precip. (mm)",               "BIO15 — precip. seasonality (CV)"))

what: Step 3 — GBIF occurrences for Sambar deer
title: Day 2a — Downloading the workshop data
text: pull fresh GBIF download Rusa unicolor Thailand. pipeline strictly filtered following basisOfRecord values retained: HUMAN_OBSERVATION OCCURRENCE OBSERVATION field-observation types relevant ENM/SDM. Fossil, machine, material-sample, preserved-specimen records excluded. Credentials. Never commit real passwords git. chunk reads environment variables; populate user .Renviron (usethis::edit_r_environ()): Restart R values loaded. variable missing, chunk falls back obvious placeholder strings source commit GitHub never reveals credentials.
code: GBIF_USER  = "your_gbif_username" GBIF_EMAIL = "your_email@example.com" GBIF_PWD   = "your_gbif_password" options(   gbif_user  = Sys.getenv("GBIF_USER",  unset = "[YOUR_GBIF_USERNAME]"),   gbif_email = Sys.getenv("GBIF_EMAIL", unset = "[YOUR_EMAIL]"),   gbif_pwd   = Sys.getenv("GBIF_PWD",   unset = "[YOUR_GBIF_PASSWORD]") )  # If no real credentials are present (e.g. fresh clone, GitHub Actions), # skip the GBIF download chunks so the document still builds. The code is # still displayed. .have_gbif_creds <- nzchar(Sys.getenv("GBIF_USER")) &&                     nzchar(Sys.getenv("GBIF_EMAIL")) &&                     nzchar(Sys.getenv("GBIF_PWD")) key <- name_backbone(name = "Rusa unicolor")$usageKey  allowed_basis <- c("HUMAN_OBSERVATION", "OCCURRENCE", "OBSERVATION")  raw_list <- lapply(allowed_basis, function(b) {   rgbif::occ_search(     taxonKey            = key,     country             = "TH",     basisOfRecord       = b,     hasCoordinate       = TRUE,     hasGeospatialIssue  = FALSE,     limit               = 5000   )$data })  raw <- dplyr::bind_rows(raw_list)  # Belt-and-braces: enforce the filter again in case the API loosened it raw <- raw %>% filter(basisOfRecord %in% allowed_basis)  write_csv(raw, file.path(gbif_dir, "sambar_gbif.csv"))  nrow(raw)

previous_headings: Step 3 — GBIF occurrences for Sambar deer
what: Clean and write the analysis-ready table
title: Day 2a — Downloading the workshop data
text: Everything Day 1, Day 2b, Day 3 need now lives data/processed/. Day 3 additionally relies local temporal_rasters/ folder, holds pre-prepared annual precipitation temperature rasters 2000–2025 (folder provided workshop materials).
code: sambar <- raw %>%   select(species,          decimalLongitude, decimalLatitude,          year, month,          basisOfRecord, gbifID) %>%   filter(!is.na(decimalLongitude),                 !is.na(decimalLatitude),                 basisOfRecord %in% allowed_basis)  sambar_sf <- st_as_sf(sambar,                           coords = c("decimalLongitude", "decimalLatitude"),                           crs    = 4326, remove = FALSE)  # Keep only points that actually fall inside Thailand inside <- st_within(sambar_sf, tha_sf, sparse = FALSE)[, 1] sambar <- sambar[inside, ]  write_csv(sambar, file.path(gbif_dir, "sambar_thailand.csv"))  plot(st_geometry(tha_sf), border = "grey20",      main = "Sambar deer occurrences") points(sambar$decimalLongitude, sambar$decimalLatitude,        pch = 21, bg = "tomato", col = "white", cex = 0.7)

title: Day 2b — Environmental thinning with bean
text: Workshop navigation — part 3 4 ENM-Thailand 2026 workshop. Day 1 — Virtual species nicheR. Build ellipsoid niche hand project . Day 2a — Downloading workshop data. Fetch Thailand, WorldClim GBIF Sambar records. Day 2b (page) — Environmental thinning bean. Reduce E-space sampling bias fitting real niche. Day 3 — Temporally-explicit SDMs TemporalModelR. Pair occurrence environment experienced. bean reduces sampling bias thinning occurrence points clustered environmental space (E-space) instead geographic space. intuition: field campaigns oversample easily reachable environments. Thinning per environmental “pod” produces even coverage species’ niche, therefore less biased fundamental-niche estimate. Today : Load cleaned GBIF occurrences Rusa unicolor Thailand (written Day2a_Data_Download.Rmd). Prepare bean visualise bias E-space. Find objective environmental grid resolution. Thin points methods (thin_env_nd stochastic, thin_env_center deterministic). Fit ellipsoid niche thinned points map suitability back G-space. Prerequisite: knit Day2a_Data_Download.Rmd first data/processed/ populated.

title: Day 2b — Environmental thinning with bean
text: clean ellipsoid use two informative bioclim variables. also map well onto Sambar deer habitat (warm, wet evergreen mixed forests).
code: cran_pkgs <- c(   "terra",   "sf",   "dplyr",   "readr",   "rmarkdown",   "knitr",   "remotes" ) to_install <- cran_pkgs[!cran_pkgs %in% rownames(installed.packages())] if (length(to_install)) install.packages(to_install, dependencies = TRUE)  # ---- bean from GitHub ------------------------------------------------------ if (!requireNamespace("bean", quietly = TRUE)) {   remotes::install_github("paanwaris/bean", upgrade = "never") } library(bean) library(terra) library(sf) library(dplyr) library(readr)  set.seed(2026) bioclim  <- rast("data/processed/bioclim/bioclim_thailand.tif") thailand <- st_read("data/processed/boundary/thailand_boundary.gpkg", quiet = TRUE)  sambar <- read_csv("data/processed/gbif/sambar_thailand.csv",                    show_col_types = FALSE)  head(sambar) env <- bioclim[[c("bio1", "bio12", "bio15")]] names(env) <- c("bio1", "bio12", "bio15")

title: Day 2b — Environmental thinning with bean
text: prepare_bean() drops rows missing coordinates, extracts environmental values occurrence, returns tidy data frame ready thinning. Look raw points G-space E-space side side. Notice clustering E-space — ’s bias bean reduce.
code: sambar_xy <- sambar %>%   dplyr::select(longitude = decimalLongitude,                 latitude  = decimalLatitude)  prepped <- prepare_bean(data        = sambar_xy,                         env_rasters = env,                         longitude   = "longitude",                         latitude    = "latitude",                         transform   = "none")  nrow(prepped) par(mfrow = c(1, 2))  plot(st_geometry(thailand), border = "grey20", main = "G-space") points(prepped[, c("longitude", "latitude")], pch = 21, bg = "tomato",        col = "white", cex = 0.7)  plot(prepped$bio1, prepped$bio12,      xlab = "BIO1 — annual mean T (°C)", ylab = "BIO12 — annual precip. (mm)",      pch = 19, col = adjustcolor("tomato", alpha.f = 0.5),      main = "E-space")  par(mfrow = c(1, 1))

what: Step 2 — Choose an objective environmental resolution
title: Day 2b — Environmental thinning with bean
text: find_env_resolution() walks grid candidate resolutions, looks average nearest-neighbour distance among points thinning, identifies “elbow” dense artificial clustering ends.
code: res_search <- find_env_resolution(data     = prepped,                                   env_vars = c("bio1", "bio12", "bio15"))  plot(res_search)  chosen_res <- res_search$suggested_resolution chosen_res

title: Day 2b — Environmental thinning with bean
text: bean ships two thinning strategies; run comparison.
code: # Stochastic: randomly retain one occurrence per "pod" thin_nd <- thin_env_nd(data            = prepped,                        env_vars        = c("bio1", "bio12", "bio15"),                        grid_resolution = chosen_res)  # Deterministic: replace each occupied pod with its centre thin_center <- thin_env_center(data            = prepped,                                env_vars        = c("bio1", "bio12", "bio15"),                                grid_resolution = c(0.5, 50, 20))  thin_nd thin_center plot_bean(   original_data  = prepped,   thinned_object = thin_nd,   env_vars       = c("bio1", "bio12", "bio15") )  plot_bean(   original_data  = prepped,   thinned_object = thin_center,   env_vars       = c("bio1", "bio12", "bio15") )

title: Day 2b — Environmental thinning with bean
text: fit_ellipsoid() formalises niche fitting bivariate (multivariate) ellipsoid around thinned points. compare ellipsoid fit raw vs. thinned data.
code: ell_raw <- fit_ellipsoid(prepped,                          env_vars = c("bio1", "bio12", "bio15"),                          method   = "covmat",                          level    = 0.95)  ell_thinned <- fit_ellipsoid(thin_nd$thinned_data,                              env_vars = c("bio1", "bio12", "bio15"),                              method   = "covmat",                              level    = 0.95)  plot(ell_raw, dims = c(1, 2)) plot(ell_raw, dims = c(1, 2, 3))  plot(ell_thinned, dims = c(1, 2)) plot(ell_thinned, dims = c(1, 2, 3))

what: Step 5 — Project the niche back to G-space
title: Day 2b — Environmental thinning with bean
code: pred_raw <- predict(ell_raw, env) pred_thinned <- predict(ell_thinned, env)  par(mfrow = c(1, 2)) plot(pred_raw$suitability, main = "Suitability — raw data") plot(pred_thinned$suitability, main = "Suitability — thinned niche") par(mfrow = c(1, 1)) dir.create("outputs", showWarnings = FALSE) terra::writeRaster(pred_thinned,                    "outputs/Day2_suitability_sambar_thinned.tif",                    overwrite = TRUE) saveRDS(ell_thinned, "outputs/Day2_ellipsoid_thinned.rds")

title: Day 2b — Environmental thinning with bean
text: Take-homes: Spatial bias occurrence data propagates E-space dense clusters. bean thins E-space fitted niche pulled toward densest sampled environments. two thinning methods (thin_env_nd vs. thin_env_center) trade randomness reproducibility — pick one stick given analysis.

title: Day 3 — Temporally-explicit SDMs with TemporalModelR
text: Workshop navigation — part 4 4 ENM-Thailand 2026 workshop. Day 1 — Virtual species nicheR. Build ellipsoid niche hand project . Day 2a — Downloading workshop data. Fetch Thailand, WorldClim GBIF Sambar records. Day 2b — Environmental thinning bean. Reduce E-space sampling bias fitting real niche. Day 3 (page) — Temporally-explicit SDMs TemporalModelR. Pair occurrence environment experienced. TemporalModelR builds temporally-explicit species distribution models. occurrence paired environmental conditions time recorded, rather long-term average. Models can project predictions time-series surfaces detect pixel-level temporal trajectories (always-suitable, increasing, decreasing, fluctuating, etc.). Data. Day 3 uses pre-prepared annual rasters already live temporal_rasters/ — one precipitation (prec_YYYY.tif) one temperature (temp_YYYY.tif) file per year 2000–2025. download new climate data ; simply read local files stack . Day 2a produced Sambar GBIF table Thailand polygon use alongside . Today : Read annual precipitation temperature rasters (2000–2025) temporal_rasters/ stack . Align common grid raster_align(), rarefy occurrences spatiotemporal_rarefaction(), extract time-matched environment temporally_explicit_extraction(). Scale rasters, build spatiotemporal CV folds, create fold-stratified pseudoabsences. Fit build_temporal_glm() build_temporal_rf() models project predictions across years generate_spatiotemporal_predictions(). Postprocess summarize_raster_outputs(), analyze_temporal_patterns(), analyze_trends_by_spatial_unit(). function names arguments follow official package docs: https://cjhughes926.github.io/TemporalModelR/articles/Preprocessing.html

title: Day 3 — Temporally-explicit SDMs with TemporalModelR
code: cran_pkgs <- c(   "terra",   "sf",   "dplyr",   "readr",   "viridis",   "randomForest",   "fastcpd",   "rmarkdown",   "knitr",   "remotes",   "geodata"   # "geodata"   # uncomment only if you enable the optional `provinces` chunk ) to_install <- cran_pkgs[!cran_pkgs %in% rownames(installed.packages())] if (length(to_install)) install.packages(to_install, dependencies = TRUE)  # ---- TemporalModelR from GitHub -------------------------------------------- if (!requireNamespace("TemporalModelR", quietly = TRUE)) {   remotes::install_github("CJHughes926/TemporalModelR") } library(TemporalModelR) library(terra) library(sf) library(dplyr) library(readr)  set.seed(2026)

title: Day 3 — Temporally-explicit SDMs with TemporalModelR
code: thailand <- st_read("data/processed/boundary/thailand_boundary.gpkg", quiet = TRUE)  sambar <- read_csv("data/processed/gbif/sambar_thailand.csv",                    show_col_types = FALSE) %>%   filter(year >= 2010, year <= 2025) %>%   rename(longitude = decimalLongitude,                 latitude  = decimalLatitude) %>%   mutate(presence = 1L)  table(sambar$year)

title: Day 3 — Temporally-explicit SDMs with TemporalModelR
text: raster_align() reprojects/resamples every raster input_dir reference writes aligned copies output_dir.
code: prec_ex <- rast("data/processed/temporal_split/prec_2025.tif") temp_ex <- rast("data/processed/temporal_split/temp_2025.tif") NDVI_ex <- rast("data/processed/temporal_split/NDVI_2025.tif")  par(mfrow = c(1, 3)) plot(prec_ex, main = "Precipitaion 2025") plot(temp_ex, main = "Temperature 2025") plot(NDVI_ex, main = "NDVI 2025")  split_dir <- "data/processed/temporal_split" aligned_dir <- "data/processed/rasters_aligned" dir.create(aligned_dir, showWarnings = FALSE, recursive = TRUE)  ref_raster <- NDVI_ex  # any one of the per-variable split rasters  raster_align(   input_dir = split_dir,   output_dir = aligned_dir,   reference_raster = ref_raster,   overwrite = TRUE )  par(mfrow = c(1, 1))

title: Day 3 — Temporally-explicit SDMs with TemporalModelR
text: Keep one record per pixel per year, repeated visit place later year preserved temporally-independent observation.
code: points_dir <- "data/processed/points" dir.create(points_dir, showWarnings = FALSE, recursive = TRUE)  points_csv <- file.path(points_dir, "sambar_temporal.csv") write_csv(sambar, points_csv)  study_crs <- st_crs(rast(ref_raster))  rare_out <- spatiotemporal_rarefaction(   points_sp = points_csv,   output_dir = file.path(points_dir, "rarefied"),   reference_raster = ref_raster,   time_cols = "year",   xcol = "longitude",   ycol = "latitude",   points_crs = study_crs,   output_prefix = "Sambar_annual" )  rare_out$input_points rare_out$spatial_points rare_out$spatiotemporal_points

what: Match each observation to the environment it experienced
title: Day 3 — Temporally-explicit SDMs with TemporalModelR
text: temporally_explicit_extraction() walks aligned raster directory using variable_patterns: clean names left, filename patterns time placeholders (YEAR) right.
code: ext_dir <- file.path(points_dir, "extracted")  ext_out <- temporally_explicit_extraction(   points_sp = rare_out$files_created$spatiotemporal,   raster_dir = aligned_dir,   variable_patterns   = c(     "prec" = "prec_YEAR",     "temp" = "temp_YEAR",     "NDVI" = "NDVI_YEAR"   ),   time_cols = "year",   xcol = "X",   ycol = "Y",   points_crs = study_crs,   output_dir = ext_dir,   output_prefix = "sambar_extracted",   save_raw = TRUE,   save_scaled = TRUE,   save_scaling_params = TRUE )  head(ext_out$raw_values) ext_out$scaling_params

title: Day 3 — Temporally-explicit SDMs with TemporalModelR
text: scale_rasters() z-scores every raster input_dir using per-variable means SDs extraction, rasters points share transformation.
code: scaled_dir <- "data/processed/rasters_scaled" dir.create(scaled_dir, showWarnings = FALSE, recursive = TRUE)  scale_rasters(   input_dir = aligned_dir,   output_dir = scaled_dir,   scaling_params_file = ext_out$files_created$scaling_params,   variable_patterns = c(     "prec" = "prec_YEAR",     "temp" = "temp_YEAR",     "NDVI" = "NDVI_YEAR"   ),   time_cols = "year",   overwrite = TRUE )

title: Day 3 — Temporally-explicit SDMs with TemporalModelR
code: par(mfrow = c(1, 3)) partition <- spatiotemporal_partition(   reference_shapefile_path = thailand,   points_file_path = ext_out$files_created$scaled,   xcol = "x",   ycol = "y",   points_crs = study_crs,   time_cols = "year",   n_spatial_folds = 2,   n_temporal_folds = 2,   max_imbalance = 0.15,   create_plot = TRUE )  partition$summary par(mfrow = c(1, 1))

title: Day 3 — Temporally-explicit SDMs with TemporalModelR
code: absences <- generate_absences(   partition_result = partition,   reference_shapefile_path = thailand,   raster_dir = scaled_dir,   variable_patterns = c(     "prec" = "prec_YEAR",     "temp" = "temp_YEAR",     "NDVI" = "NDVI_YEAR"   ),   method = "buffer",   buffer_distance = 50000,   # 10 km — coarser pixels need a bigger buffer   ratio = 2,   time_cols = "year",   create_plot = TRUE,   plot_by_fold = TRUE,   verbose = FALSE )  absences$summary

what: GLM (with a quadratic term on temperature)
title: Day 3 — Temporally-explicit SDMs with TemporalModelR
code: glm_res <- build_temporal_glm(   partition_result = partition,   pseudoabsence_result = absences,   model_formula = ~ temp + I(temp^2) + prec + NDVI,   link = "logit",   threshold_method = "tss",   output_dir = "outputs/GLM_Models",   create_plot = FALSE,   overwrite = TRUE,   time_cols = "year",   verbose = FALSE )  glm_res$fold_test_metrics

title: Day 3 — Temporally-explicit SDMs with TemporalModelR
code: rf_res <- build_temporal_rf(   partition_result = partition,   pseudoabsence_result = absences,   model_vars = c("temp", "prec", "NDVI"),   rf_params = list(ntree = 500),   threshold_method = "tss",   output_dir = "outputs/RF_Models",   create_plot = FALSE,   overwrite = TRUE,   time_cols = "year" )  rf_res$fold_test_metrics

title: Day 3 — Temporally-explicit SDMs with TemporalModelR
text: Quick look three years RF model:
code: time_steps <- data.frame(year = c(2010:2025))  glm_preds <- generate_spatiotemporal_predictions(   partition_result = partition,   model_result = glm_res,   pseudoabsence_result = absences,   raster_dir = scaled_dir,   variable_patterns = c("prec" = "prec_YEAR",                         "temp" = "temp_YEAR",                         "NDVI" = "NDVI_YEAR"),    time_cols = "year",   time_steps = time_steps,   output_dir = "outputs/glm_predictions",   overwrite = TRUE )  rf_preds <- generate_spatiotemporal_predictions(   partition_result = partition,   model_result = rf_res,   pseudoabsence_result = absences,   raster_dir = scaled_dir,   variable_patterns = c("prec" = "prec_YEAR",                         "temp" = "temp_YEAR",                         "NDVI" = "NDVI_YEAR"),    time_cols = "year",   time_steps = time_steps,   output_dir = "outputs/rf_predictions",   overwrite = TRUE ) sample_years <- c(2000, 2010, 2015, 2020, 2025) rf_files <- list.files("outputs/rf_predictions",                        pattern = paste0("Prediction_.*(",                                         paste(sample_years, collapse = "|"),                                         ").*\\.tif$"),                        full.names = TRUE)  rf_stack <- terra::rast(rf_files[seq_len(min(5, length(rf_files)))])  par(mfrow = c(1, terra::nlyr(rf_stack))) for (i in seq_len(terra::nlyr(rf_stack))) {   plot(rf_stack[[i]],        main = names(rf_stack)[i])   plot(sf::st_geometry(thailand), add = TRUE, border = "grey20") } par(mfrow = c(1, 1))

title: Day 3 — Temporally-explicit SDMs with TemporalModelR
text: summarize_raster_outputs() reads per-time-step vote-count rasters prediction directory applies consensus threshold.
code: consensus <- summarize_raster_outputs(   predictions_dir = "outputs/rf_predictions",   output_dir = "outputs/rf_consensus",   overwrite = TRUE )  plot(consensus$frequency_raster,      main = "Proportion of time steps each pixel was suitable (RF)")

title: Day 3 — Temporally-explicit SDMs with TemporalModelR
text: analyze_temporal_patterns() runs fastcpd per pixel classifies trajectories never-suitable, always-suitable, -pattern, increasing, decreasing, fluctuating.
code: patterns_out <- analyze_temporal_patterns(   binary_stack = consensus$consensus_stack,   summary_raster = consensus$frequency_raster,   time_steps = time_steps,   output_dir = "outputs/rf_patterns",   spatial_autocorrelation = FALSE,   estimate_time = FALSE,   overwrite = TRUE )  plot(patterns_out$pattern,      main = "Temporal trajectory class per pixel",      type = "classes")

title: Day 3 — Temporally-explicit SDMs with TemporalModelR
code: provinces <- geodata::gadm(country = "THA", level = 1, path = "data/raw") |>   st_as_sf()  regional <- analyze_trends_by_spatial_unit(   shapefile_path = provinces,   name_field = "NAME_1",   binary_stack = consensus$consensus_stack,   pattern_raster = patterns_out$pattern,   time_decrease_raster = patterns_out$time_decrease,   time_increase_raster = patterns_out$time_increase,   time_steps = time_steps,   output_dir = "outputs/rf_regional",   overwrite = TRUE,   create_plot = TRUE )  head(regional$overall_summary) regional$plots$time_series regional$plots$annual_change regional$plots$total_change_by_unit

title: Day 3 — Temporally-explicit SDMs with TemporalModelR
text: covered: Read pre-prepared annual precipitation, temperature rasters, NDVI (2000–2025) directly temporal_rasters/ stacked — new downloads. Built fully temporally-explicit pipeline: time-stamped occurrences ↔︎ time-stamped rasters. Used spatio-temporal cross-validation folds fold-stratified pseudoabsences. Fit GLM RF temporal models projected time. Classified pixel-level temporal trajectories aggregated region.
